Rung.Comment:
============================================================ L_MUL : 64-bit X 32-bit Multiply (64-bit result) ============================================================ Revision 1.0-00 Release: 2009-Jul-31 Initial Release ============================================================ Multiplies a LINT (signed 64-bit) integer by a DINT (signed 32-bit) integer and returns a LINT (signed 64-bit) product.
This instruction breaks the LINT input into five DINTs (13, 13, 13, 13 and 12 bits) to do the math in order to avoid generating a Controller MINOR FAULT on overflow. The controller must take time to log the minor faults, and many users do not want to see Minor Faults on normal math operations. See the RSLogix 5000 Help Topic "Arithmetic Status Flags" for more information.) =================================================== CONVERT LINT INPUT TO DINTs =================================================== This COPy takes the input LINT and breaks it into a pair of DINTs so we can do math on it. Logix V17 has a LINT data type, but math instructions (and AOI Input and Output Parameters) and bitwise (BTD) instructions don't support LINT.

=================================================== TRACK SIGNS =================================================== If the LINT input is negative, track that, and negate it. If the DINT input is negative, track that, and negate it. We'll do the multiplication with the unsigned values, then figure out the sign of the product.
Rung.Text: [XIC(Wrk_InpA[1].31) [NOT(Wrk_InpA[0],Wrk_InpA[0]) NOT(Wrk_InpA[1],Wrk_InpA[1]) ,EQU(Wrk_InpA[0],16#7FFF_FFFF) MOV(16#8000_0000,Wrk_InpA[0]) OTE(Wrk_InpALoOvflw) ,XIO(Wrk_InpALoOvflw) ADD(Wrk_InpA[0],1,Wrk_InpA[0]) ,EQU(Wrk_InpA[0],0) [EQU(Wrk_InpA[1],16#7FFF_FFFF) MOV(16#8000_0000,Wrk_InpA[1]) OTE(Wrk_InpAHiOvflw) ,XIO(Wrk_InpAHiOvflw) ADD(Wrk_InpA[1],1,Wrk_InpA[1]) ] ,OTE(Wrk_A_Sign) ] ,ABS(Inp_B,Wrk_InpB) XIC(Inp_B.31) OTE(Wrk_B_Sign) ];

Rung.Comment:
=================================================== SPREAD 64 BITS ACROSS FIVE DINTs =================================================== If we multiply 16 bits by 16 bits to get 32, the math may overflow, setting the S:V status bit and THROWING A MINOR FAULT. (We want to do unsigned multiplicaiton, but DINTs are signed...)
So we multiply 13 bits by 13 bits to get 26 and then build the partial sums.
Doing the 13x13 -> 26 bit MULs and 26+26 -> 27 bit ADDs ensures we never have an Overflow that throws a Minor Fault. The BTDs split the 64 bits of Input A among the fjve DINTs used in the math.
We need to do the same with our 32-bit input, because we will be doing "elementary school place value multiplication" in 13-bit groups.

Rung.Text: [MUL(Wrk_A[0],Wrk_B[0],Wrk_CX) MUL(Wrk_A[1],Wrk_B[0],Wrk_DX) MUL(Wrk_A[2],Wrk_B[0],Wrk_EX) MUL(Wrk_A[3],Wrk_B[0],Wrk_FX) MUL(Wrk_A[4],Wrk_B[0],Wrk_GX) ,MUL(Wrk_A[0],Wrk_B[1],Wrk_CY) MUL(Wrk_A[1],Wrk_B[1],Wrk_DY) MUL(Wrk_A[2],Wrk_B[1],Wrk_EY) MUL(Wrk_A[3],Wrk_B[1],Wrk_FY) ,MUL(Wrk_A[0],Wrk_B[2],Wrk_CZ) MUL(Wrk_A[1],Wrk_B[2],Wrk_DZ) MUL(Wrk_A[2],Wrk_B[2],Wrk_EZ) ];

Rung.Comment: =================================================== DETERMINE SIGN OF FINAL PRODUCT =================================================== If only one of the Inputs is negative, negate the product.
Rung.Text: [XIC(Wrk_A_Sign) XIO(Wrk_B_Sign) ,XIO(Wrk_A_Sign) XIC(Wrk_B_Sign) ][NOT(Wrk_Out[0],Wrk_Out[0]) NOT(Wrk_Out[1],Wrk_Out[1]) ,EQU(Wrk_Out[0],16#7FFF_FFFF) MOV(16#8000_0000,Wrk_Out[0]) OTE(Wrk_OutLoOvflw) ,XIO(Wrk_OutLoOvflw) ADD(Wrk_Out[0],1,Wrk_Out[0]) ,EQU(Wrk_Out[0],0) [EQU(Wrk_Out[1],16#7FFF_FFFF) MOV(16#8000_0000,Wrk_Out[1]) OTE(Wrk_OutHiOvflw) ,XIO(Wrk_OutHiOvflw) ADD(Wrk_Out[1],1,Wrk_Out[1]) ] ];
